description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service, a global before_script that runs docker version, docker info and docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com, defines DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2 and GL_CONTAINER=registry.gitlab.com/mattmatters/opencv-wasm variables, and sets a cache keyed by $CI_PIPELINE_ID with untracked files. In the build stage add three jobs—build:devserver, build:e2e, and build:wasm—each executing docker build -t $GL_CONTAINER/<name>:$CI_PIPELINE_ID -f docker/Dockerfile_<name> . followed by docker push $GL_CONTAINER/<name>:$CI_PIPELINE_ID. In the release stage add release:js (using python:latest and overriding before_script with echo "overwrite before_script") that runs bash ./scripts/build_static.sh and saves the dist/ directory as an artifact, and release:wasm that pulls $GL_CONTAINER/wasm:$CI_PIPELINE_ID, creates a dist directory, runs the container mounting the host dist path, and also archives dist/ as an artifact. In the test stage add test:e2e, dependent on release:js and release:wasm, which pulls the devserver and e2e images, creates a Docker network, runs the devserver container mounting $(pwd)/dist, sleeps, runs the e2e container with BrowserStack credentials and ADDRESS env, checks the container exit code to echo "passed" or fail, and finally stops the devserver container. In the deploy stage (only on master) using python:3.6, install awscli, then execute bash scripts/deploy.sh to deploy the built artifacts to the S3 bucket s3://opencvwasm, with dependencies on release:js and release:wasm.
